research.status_summary: Actively hiring and aggressively expanding — finalizing a ~$300M round at a $12B valuation (June 2, 2026), just five months after its $400M Series F at $9B, with no layoffs or acquisition of the company itself reported.
research.hiring_policy: Cyera lists 20+ remote-friendly roles and explicitly advertises remote work with office-setup reimbursement; no confirmed EOR policy found, and visa sponsorship is not prominently listed in postings — likely US-entity hire-only for most roles.
recent_news: June 2, 2026: Cyera finalizing a $300M round led by Evolution Equity Partners at a $12B valuation — bringing total capital raised to 2B+; valued at 80x ARR despite not yet being profitable.
May 2026: Acquired Israeli startup Genie Security (~$50M), one month after acquiring Ryft (~$100-130M) to add AI-ready data lake capabilities; also added Steffan Tomlinson to its board (May 12).

April 2026: Acquired Ryft Data Inc.; CEO Yotam Segev stated in Calcalist interview that Cyera aims to become 'the CrowdStrike or Palo Alto Networks of data security'; also recognized as Leader in Sensitive Data Discovery & Classification.
research.growth_signal: Headcount reached ~1,462 as of April 30, 2026 (up ~500 hires YTD per PitchBook), ARR surpassed $150M, revenue grew 3.4x YoY, and valuation has tripled in under 12 months — one of the fastest-scaling Israeli cybersecurity companies ever.
research.fit_for_lior: Cyera is a strong match on the AI-governance and data-security product axis — roles like Marketing Ops AI Manager, Senior Business Value Analyst, and Product Marketing align with Lior's Product Analyst / Vibe Coder threads, and the company's narrative around 'AI without guardrails = risk' maps well to Creative AI positioning.
